title: andromeda polifolia subsp. glaucophylla
intro: Andromeda polifolia subsp. glaucophylla, also known as bog rosemary, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is a shrub that grows in acidic, wet environments, such as bogs and heathlands, and is native to North America. The medicinal properties of bog rosemary include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues, including wounds, infections, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat stress and anxiety, improve circulation, and boost the immune system.
